delivery.raw: 主に対面（全開講回数の過半数）、一部オンライン

summary: This seminar focuses on research into specific aspects of Intercultural Communication, Comparative Culture, English Language use in context and gender. This seminar builds toward development of research ideas and preparation of an undergraduate thesis proposal, which should include a scholarly review of literature, method of research, and data collection leading to a final thesis product written in English. Students will be expected to decide on their thesis topic, prepare a review of literature related to their theme and present on this in the seminar. The review of literature related to selected thesis topic will be submitted in the form of an annotated bibliography at the end of the semester.
aims: This seminar explores themes related to the study of Intercultural Communication, Comparative Culture, and Applied Linguistics, primarily as they apply to language in use. The seminar examines differing communicative patterns and strategies between cultures. Seminar IV focuses on reporting research and building academic writing skills through various assigned writing projects specifically related to individual student interests. We will also explore Gender from a comparative culture perspective.
goals:
Develop process approach to academic writing
Acquire basic skills for conducting professional research
Develop skills for comparing culture through critical analysis
